description: Phase 70 - Universal Tool Layer: Create packages/quant-tools with typed tool registry, per-app tool definitions (17 apps x 5+ tools each), multi-tool planner, permission engine, MCP server adapter, intent router, undo registry, audit log.
steps: Step 1 - Create packages/quant-tools/package.json: Name @quant/quant-tools, workspace:* dep on @quant/common. Include typecheck/test/build/lint scripts. Create tsconfig.json following existing package pattern.

Step 2 - Create src/types.ts with comprehensive tool type definitions: ToolDefinition (id, appId, name, description, inputSchema with typed Record, outputSchema, permissionTier 0-3, costEstimate, undoRecipe, tags). PermissionTier enum (Auto=0, Notify=1, Confirm=2, Admin=3). ToolExecutionContext, ToolResult, ToolChain, ToolPlan, ToolPlanStep, IntentMatch, AuditEntry, UndoAction, MCPToolCatalog interfaces.

Step 3 - Create src/registry/tool-registry.ts: ToolRegistry class with register(tool), unregister(id), get(id), getByApp(appId), search(query), listAll(), validateInput(toolId, args), getPermissionTier(toolId) methods. Uses Map internally. Supports filtering by app, permission tier, tags.

Step 4 - Create src/tools/ directory with per-app tool definitions. Each file exports an array of ToolDefinition objects. Create: mail-tools.ts (send, search, archive, label, draft - 5 tools), chat-tools.ts (send, create-channel, react, pin, search - 5), calendar-tools.ts (create-event, reschedule, invite, cancel, list-today - 5), docs-tools.ts (create, edit, share, export, search - 5), drive-tools.ts (upload, download, share, organize, search - 5), meet-tools.ts (create, join, record, end, schedule - 5), neon-tools.ts (post, like, comment, repost, search - 5), sync-tools.ts (sync-contacts, sync-files, sync-settings, check-status, resolve-conflict - 5), tube-tools.ts (upload, publish, schedule, analytics, moderate - 5), max-tools.ts (start-session, end-session, generate, analyze, explain - 5), edits-tools.ts (create-project, render, add-track, export, apply-effect - 5), ads-tools.ts (create-campaign, set-budget, target-audience, pause, analytics - 5), maps-tools.ts (search, directions, share-location, save-place, distance - 5), photos-tools.ts (upload, organize, edit, share, search - 5), device-tools.ts (control, status, configure, schedule-action, emergency - 5), studio-tools.ts (create-app, publish, test, deploy, monitor - 5), payments-tools.ts (send, request, invoice, refund, balance - 5). Total: 85 typed tools.

Step 5 - Create src/planner/intent-router.ts: IntentRouter class with route(naturalLanguageInput) method that parses text to identify required tools and parameters. Uses keyword matching and pattern recognition. Returns IntentMatch[] with toolId, confidence, extractedParams.

Step 6 - Create src/planner/multi-step-planner.ts: MultiStepPlanner class with plan(intent, context) method that decomposes complex intents into ordered ToolPlanStep[]. Handles data dependencies between steps (output of step N feeds into step N+1). Has validatePlan() and estimateCost() methods.

Step 7 - Create src/executor/tool-executor.ts: ToolExecutor class with execute(plan, context) method. Runs steps sequentially, passes state between them. Handles errors with retry logic. Records execution audit trail. Supports dry-run mode.

Step 8 - Create src/permissions/permission-engine.ts: PermissionEngine class with evaluate(toolId, user, context) method returning {allowed, reason, confirmationRequired}. Implements tier-based logic: tier 0=auto-execute, tier 1=notify user, tier 2=require confirmation, tier 3=require admin approval. Has costPreview(plan) method.

Step 9 - Create src/undo/undo-registry.ts: UndoRegistry class with recordAction(executionId, undoRecipe), canUndo(executionId), executeUndo(executionId) methods. Stores undo recipes with TTL. Supports chained undo for multi-step executions.

Step 10 - Create src/audit/audit-log.ts: AuditLog class with log(entry), query(filters), getByExecution(id), getByUser(userId), getByTool(toolId) methods. Records all tool invocations with timestamps, results, permissions checked.

Step 11 - Create src/mcp/mcp-server.ts: MCPServerAdapter class that exposes quant-tools via MCP protocol. Has getCatalog() returning tool listing, handleRequest(toolName, args) method, authenticate(token) method.
Step 12 - Create src/index.ts exporting all public APIs from registry, planner, executor, permissions, undo, audit, mcp, and tool definitions.
Step 13 - Create src/__tests__/tool-registry.test.ts with at least 10 tests: register/unregister, get by app, search, validate input, permission tier checks.
Step 14 - Create src/__tests__/planner.test.ts with at least 8 tests: intent routing, multi-step planning, data dependency resolution, cost estimation.
Step 15 - Create src/__tests__/executor.test.ts with at least 8 tests: sequential execution, error handling, dry-run, audit trail creation.
Step 16 - Create src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts with at least 6 tests: tier evaluation, confirmation flow, cost preview.
acceptance_criteria: packages/quant-tools/ is a complete package with 85+ typed tool definitions across 17 app-specific files
ToolRegistry registers and queries tools correctly
IntentRouter parses natural language to tool matches
MultiStepPlanner creates executable plans from complex intents
ToolExecutor runs plans with state passing between steps
UndoRegistry stores and executes undo recipes
MCPServerAdapter exposes tools for external clients
At least 32 tests pass across all test files
verification: pnpm typecheck - should pass with 0 errors including new quant-tools package
pnpm test - should pass including 32+ new tests in quant-tools
findings: All 47 tests pass (16 registry, 14 planner, 9 executor, 8 permissions). Prettier reformatted files via lint-staged during commit. Full monorepo typecheck (114 tasks) passes. Package has 85 tools across 17 app-specific files.
